I just want you to see that this is what happens. You see, you start talking and everybody stares at the back of your head. >> >> The first one we mentioned that a woman would be a slave taken as a prisoner of war by the mighty armies of Islam and her children would be would be free by default and so they would be born emancipated even if she remains a slave and this is an indicator that Islam would stretch in its empire and in its reach. So that happened. The second one that due to the muddling of of lineage and due to the fact that people are not keeping track of their lineage. A woman may be purchased by her own son or daughter as a slave literally not knowing that this is the mother not knowing that this is the daughter or son and this occurred as well. Those are two. The third is figurative, right? that a woman would be a figurative slave to her child who is figuratively her master. Meaning the child would behave and fill the role of master and the parent will fulfill the role of slave dutiful dutiful servant. Why? due to the overreach of tyrannical governments near the end of time and the breaking up of the family unit. Correct? This as well happened. I mean the time we're living in is is is pretty indicative of that. So these are three meanings. The next one he mentions, he says the second one he mentions is that you would observe the barefoot, naked and poor, impoverished shepherds of sheep competing with one another in building their high buildings. This, if it were true, which it's not, but if it were really true, is not indicative of true wealth. Wealth is something that has to be brought up generationwise. It has to be built up over generations. This is why it is incredibly rare in the previous that somebody would come from a a poor family or what we call middle class family and become obscenely rich. This is almost unheard of because it is momentum. It's trying to push something that's very heavy. You have to get it started and it takes a long time. It takes generations to build up wealth. summary: This lecture discusses Islamic teachings on the signs of the Day of Judgment (Yawm al-Qiyamah) based on a hadith narrated by Omar ibn al-Khattab. The speaker explains two major signs mentioned by the Prophet Muhammad: first, a slave girl giving birth to her master, which has three valid interpretations including literal enslavement during Islamic conquests, confusion of lineage, and figurative child-parent role reversals due to societal breakdown. Second, the speaker elaborates on barefoot shepherds suddenly acquiring obscene wealth and competing in building skyscrapers, analyzing this as a sign of rigged economies, rapid wealth accumulation without generational foundation, and the dangerous combination of ignorance with power. The lecture concludes by identifying the questioner as the angel Gabriel (Jibril), explaining how the Prophet deliberately delayed revealing this identity for three days to build students' curiosity and deeper engagement with the knowledge.
suggested_title: Signs of the Day of Judgment: Slave Girl Born to Master and Barefoot Shepherds Building Skyscrapers
catchy_title: When Shepherds Build Skyscrapers: Signs of Qiyamah
